key_insight: A least-squares weak Galerkin formulation turns the Stokes saddle-point problem into an SPD system while preserving optimal convergence on general polygonal/polyhedral meshes.
engineering_relevance: This makes incompressible flow simulation more robust for complex geometries by avoiding the inf-sup constraint and simplifying linear solver implementation.
method: Use discontinuous polynomial spaces with discrete weak derivatives.
Formulate Stokes as a least-squares weak Galerkin finite element problem.
Employ degree k velocity and degree k-1 pressure to obtain optimal error rates.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports theoretical well-posedness and optimal-order error estimates, and states that numerical experiments confirm the predicted convergence rates.
watch_item: Although the SPD system is easier to solve, accurate implementation still depends on careful construction of weak derivatives and mesh-dependent discrete norms on arbitrary polyhedral partitions.
paper_title: Solving the Stokes Equations via a Least Squares Weak Galerkin Method
